summary: Excellent early‑67 soundboard capture with a blistering New Potato, soaring Lovelight and a legendary first “That’s It” suite.
review: The show is hailed as one of the most energetic snapshots of the Dead’s psychedelic spring ’67, highlighted by a monster New Potato Caboose, an electrifying Lovelight and the debut ‘That’s It’ (The Other One) suite.  The soundboard source is generally clear and lively, though a few reviewers note occasional drop‑outs and the infamous cut‑off in the middle of Lovelight.  Overall the performance and recording are praised as must‑listen for any Deadhead.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews, most 4–5 stars, consistently praise the musical performance and only minor recording flaws; show quality dominates the rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, lively mix typical of a 1967 soundboard.  Several reviewers mention occasional drop‑outs and a cut in Lovelight, but overall the tape is considered listenable and vivid.

show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy psychedelic set that swings from fast, bluesy numbers (Hurts Me Too, Cold Rain & Snow) into extended jams (New Potato, The Other One), with a brief Lovelight cut that still shines despite the splice.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Fire‑filled guitar work praised; occasional stumbles noted but overall his solo is described as “blazing” and central to the New Potato.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass praised on Beat It and for tight counterpoint with Jerry during New Potato; solid, melodic foundation.
band_member_comments.Bob: Pigpen’s vocal power highlighted on Lovelight and It Hurts Me Too; called “great” and a key blues voice.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and newly‑added Mickey Hart noted as locking in smoothly; drumming described as pliable and supportive of the jams.
